contextual.title: Inhaled Corticosteroids in U-5 Children With Acute Respiratory Infection in Uganda: A Randomised Trial
contextual.text:
Summary: The purpose of this study is to find out whether adjunct treatment with inhaled corticosteroids lead to faster improvement and reduce mortality of children under 5 years of age admitted to hospital with ALRI.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 children aged 2 to 59 months with cough and or difficult breathing 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 Presence of a serious concurrent illness such as meningitis, Children with congenital or acquired heart disease Severe anaemia Measles pneumonia Foreign body inhalation A confirmed diagnosis of pulmonary tuberculosis

Bacterial Pneumonia.definition: Inflammation of the lung parenchyma that is caused by bacterial infections.

Viral Pneumonia.definition: Inflammation of the lung parenchyma that is caused by a viral infection.
